I did not want to accept this precious old article but she insisted. for me to have it. I know know that it will be lovely taken care of. Johanna E. Kocke 1985

dc-record.title: Bowl, Dessert
dc-record.description: Dessert bowl from the San Francisco Palace Hotel found in a "stump dump". Stump dumps were hollowed redwood stumps that were used for discarding garbage. This plate was located whole in a burned out redwood stump near Huckleberry Heights next to train tracks approximately miles from River Road and 6 miles from Cazadero Stump dumps were common in the early 1900s.
dc-record.subject: Bowl, Dessert

dc-record.title: Dish, Serving
dc-record.description: A small serving dish from the San Francisco Palace Hotel found in a "stump dump". Stump dumps were hollowed redwood stumps that were used for discarding garbage. This plate was located in three pieces in a burned out redwood stump near Huckleberry Heights next to train tracks approximately miles from River Road and 6 miles from Cazadero Stump dumps were common in the early 1900s.
dc-record.subject: Dish, Serving

dc-record.title: Canoe
dc-record.description: Easdale Canoe. Constructed by John and David Easdale at their ship-building workshop in Guernewood Park, Guerneville, CA (current site of Ferrellgas). Original was canvas with star on hull indicating it was an "Easdale". Easdales were created from the 20's until the 50's. The iron keel on this boat was installed by Clare Harris, as part of the Rio Nido fleet of rental canoes. The original cane seat has been replaced. The original canvas has been covered and the star obscured by paint. Donated by Monte Rio Rec & Park in 2007. Long-term Loan document in file.
dc-record.subject: Canoe
Canoes
Easdale
John & Dale Easdale
dc-record.creator: John & Dale Easdale

dc-record.title: Blue Bird View
Painting
dc-record.description:
Framed watercolor, Painter Bevery Maris, 1999 Painting description: Four men sitting in front of wooden building. Model T car with "CASINO" sign in center of painting with four passengers and two men standing alongside the car.
card taped to painting: Blue Bird View: At Monte Rio, four minute walk from Northwestern Pacific R.R. depot. Ideally located among the redwoods; above the rRussian River and within sight of the famous bathing beach. Completely furnished 2, 3 and 4 room apartments fully equipped for housekeeping; electric lights and running water. Also furnished hoiusekeeping tents with outside kitchens and electric lights. Situated on the bank of the river under shade trees. Terms $8 and up per week. For further information appy to John Schmidt,
dc-record.subject: Painting
Blue Bird View
Casino

dc-record.title: Cornet
dc-record.description:
1800's cornet. Wendell Joost was the town's Funeral Director for 12 years (1977 - 1989). He founf the cornet at Fif Creek Antiques and Collectables. (The building was the Pedoria Auto Part Store).
The cornet was brought un by the family of Roscoe Drake. When Wendall was the funeral director, Roscoe would play taps at the funeral of veterans who had passed away at Redwood Memorial Gardens in Guerneville.

dc-record.description: 8.5 x 10.5 - computer print in a wooden frame of images of Colonel James Boydston Armstrong daughter , Lizzie Armstrong (Marries Parson Jones) at a young age and older, Kate Armstrong, Parson Jones. Lizzie negotiated the final sale of Armstrong Woods, fullfilling her father's life long dream. Colonel Armstrong, close friends with Luther Burbank, envisioned a botanical garden and aboretum.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic

Parson Jones

Ideally located aog the redwods: above the Russian Rivr and within sight of the famous beach. Completely furnishe 2, 3 and 4 room aparments fully equipped for housekeeping; eletric lighs and running water. Also furnished housekeeping tents without outside kitchens and electic lights. Situated on bank of river under shade trees. Ters $9 and up per week. For further information apply to John Schmidt, Box 206, Monte Rio. Sonoma County, Cal. Source: NWPRR Vacation Tour Book
dc-record.subject: Painting

dc-record.title: Print, Photographic
dc-record.description: Framed print, enlarged from original postcard. Monte Rio's Pink Elephant, began as a grocery and evolved into a bar and cafe afer the repeal of prohibition. During the flood of 1940, patrons rowed their boats to the front door and demanded a last round before evacuating. Photo shows the Pink Elephant, the next door laundry, Golden State Ice Cream, with sign "Niclaes' Bungalow". Signs on Pink Elephant: Restaurant Meals at all Hours; the neon lighted Pink Elephant sign, a sign over the front door "Radio Grab Bag Show Broadcast Here Sat. 8:30 m, Come join the fun" Two cars and a truck with sign "Arrow" from late 1940's are parked diagonally in front of the Pink.
dc-record.subject: Print, Photographic
Patterson Point Preserve
Villa Grande
